messages.content: Can you explain in simple terms why ice floats on water instead of sinking, when most solids are denser than their liquid form?
What's the best way to keep guacamole from turning brown if I want to make it a few hours ahead of a party? I've tried adding lime juice but it still seems to discolor pretty fast once it sits out.

I'm planning a two-week trip through Japan in early April and want to catch cherry blossom season without dealing with insane crowds the whole time. Are there any lesser-known cities or regions outside of Tokyo and Kyoto that still have great hanami spots but a more relaxed pace?
What actually caused the fall of the Roman Empire? I've heard so many different theories and I'm curious which explanation historians consider the most credible today.
Can you suggest a punchier opening line for a short story about a lighthouse keeper who discovers a message in a bottle?
If I invest 5000 dollars at a 6 percent annual interest rate compounded monthly, roughly how much will it be worth after 10 years?

My roommate keeps leaving dirty dishes in the sink for days even after I've asked her nicely multiple times. I don't want to start a huge fight but I'm getting really frustrated. What's a good way to bring this up again without it turning into a blowout argument?
What's the actual difference between RAM and storage on a laptop? My cousin keeps telling me to buy more RAM but the laptop I'm looking at already has plenty of storage space.
Is it true that cracking your knuckles all the time can cause arthritis later in life, or is that just an old wives' tale my grandmother used to repeat whenever I did it as a kid?

I just got my first full-time job and I'm not sure how much of my paycheck I should be putting into a retirement account versus just saving in a regular bank account. What's a reasonable percentage to aim for when you're only 23 and still paying off a bit of student debt?
Why do soccer matches sometimes end in a draw while other tournament games go to penalty shootouts instead? What determines which format a given competition uses?

Can you recommend a few underrated sci-fi movies from the 90s that don't get talked about as much as the big blockbusters? I've already seen the usual classics and I'm looking for something a little more obscure to watch this weekend.
How do you know when it's the right time to introduce someone you're dating to your parents? We've only been together for about two months but it feels pretty serious already.
My tomato plants keep getting yellow leaves near the bottom even though I water them regularly. Could this be a sign of overwatering or is it more likely a nutrient deficiency?
Is it normal for a cat to sleep more than sixteen hours a day, or should I be worried and take her to the vet?

What's the easiest Romance language to pick up for an English speaker who already knows a bit of Spanish? I'm thinking about learning either Italian or Portuguese next but I'm not sure how different the grammar really is.

Do you think free will actually exists, or is everything we do essentially just the result of prior causes we don't have control over? I've been reading about determinism lately and it's honestly kind of messing with how I think about daily decisions.
What are some good beginner-friendly songs to learn on acoustic guitar that don't require complicated chord changes or fast finger picking?

I'm trying to get into watercolor painting as a complete beginner and I'm overwhelmed by all the paper and paint options at the art store. What's a solid, affordable starter set that won't make basic techniques like washes and blending frustrating to practice with?

I've been at my current job for almost four years and I feel like I've plateaued, but I'm nervous about switching industries entirely at 29. Is it generally seen as a red flag by employers if your next job is completely unrelated to your previous work experience, or does that matter less than I think, especially if I can clearly explain how my existing skills would actually transfer over during interviews?
At what age is it reasonable to start giving a kid a small allowance, and should it be tied to chores around the house or just given regardless of whether they help out?
What's the best way to patch a small hole in drywall left over from a picture hook, without it being obvious once I repaint the wall?
Why do some trees lose their leaves in autumn while evergreens like pine trees stay green and keep their needles all year round?
Is it actually healthier to eat several small meals throughout the day instead of three bigger ones, or is that mostly a myth that doesn't really affect metabolism much?
Why does Russia span eleven time zones while a country like China only uses a single time zone despite being nearly as wide?

Why do we sometimes remember embarrassing moments from years ago more vividly than important things that happened last week? It feels like my brain replays cringey memories at random times for no reason, and I'm curious if there's an actual explanation for why that happens so often.
Is it worth paying for an online coding bootcamp, or can someone realistically become job-ready as a developer just by using free resources and building their own projects?

How much of a real difference does individual recycling actually make compared to the impact of large corporations and industrial pollution? Sometimes it feels like sorting my plastics is pointless when a handful of companies are responsible for most global emissions.
What makes Pride and Prejudice still so widely read and loved two centuries after it was published? I recently started it and I'm curious what keeps drawing new generations of readers to it.

I'm learning Python and keep getting confused about when to use a list versus a dictionary for storing data. Is there a simple rule of thumb for deciding which one makes more sense for a given problem, especially when I'm just starting out and everything feels interchangeable?

How do vaccines actually train the immune system to fight off diseases it hasn't encountered yet? I understand the basic idea of exposing the body to a weakened or inactive version of a pathogen, but I don't really get how that translates into long-term protection years later, sometimes even decades after the original shot, especially when the actual virus circulating by then has mutated quite a bit from the original strain.
What's a quick way to tell if chicken breast is fully cooked without cutting it open and drying it out?
Is it worth renting a car for a week in Iceland, or is public transportation and organized tours enough to see the major sights comfortably?

Why did the printing press have such a massive impact on European society in the 15th century? I understand it made books cheaper to produce, but I'm curious about the specific social and political changes historians point to as direct results of its invention.
How do professional authors usually deal with writer's block? I'm halfway through a novel draft and have completely lost momentum, and pushing through feels forced no matter how much I try.

Can you explain the Monty Hall problem in a way that actually makes intuitive sense? I understand the math says you should switch doors, but it still feels wrong every time I think about it, like the odds shouldn't change once one door is revealed.
How do I politely decline a wedding invitation from a coworker I'm not particularly close with without seeming rude about it?

Why do some websites load almost instantly while others with seemingly similar content take several seconds? Is that mostly about server location, image sizes, or something else entirely that I'm not thinking of?
Is it better to stretch before a workout or after one, and does it actually make a meaningful difference in preventing injury either way?
What's the real difference between a Roth IRA and a traditional IRA when it comes to taxes, both now and after retirement?

Why do some basketball players seem to get so much better at three-point shooting later in their careers, sometimes after being known as poor shooters early on? Is that mostly due to changes in coaching and shot mechanics, or is it more about confidence and repetition building up over years of practice, and do teams actually track this kind of long-term shooting improvement statistically or is it mostly anecdotal commentary from broadcasters?
What's a good animated movie to watch with a group of adults that isn't just aimed at little kids and has some real emotional depth?

My best friend and I have started drifting apart since she moved to a different city last year, and our conversations feel more forced than they used to. Is this just a normal part of friendships changing over time, or is there something I should be doing differently to keep us close?
What herbs are easiest to grow indoors on a kitchen windowsill for someone with no gardening experience at all and not much natural light?
Why do dogs tilt their heads when you talk to them in a certain tone of voice? My puppy does it constantly and it's adorable, but I'm curious if there's an actual reason behind the behavior.
Why does English have so many silent letters in words like knight and though compared to other European languages with more phonetic spelling?
What's the actual difference between stoicism and just suppressing your emotions? They seem similar on the surface but I assume there's more nuance.

Why do certain chord progressions, like the one used in so many pop songs, sound so satisfying to our ears across pretty much every genre? Is there an actual musical or psychological explanation for why some combinations of notes just feel right to most listeners?
What's the difference between impressionism and post-impressionism in painting? I always mix up the two movements when I'm at a museum.
How long should I wait before following up after a job interview if I haven't heard back yet, and what's the best way to phrase it?
